title: Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib is a Sikh gurdwara located in Kiratpur, Punjab, India that is a popular place for dispersing ashes for Sikhs as part of the Antam Sanskar funerary rites of
overview.description: Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib is a Sikh gurdwara located in Kiratpur, Punjab, India that is a popular place for dispersing ashes for Sikhs as part of the Antam Sanskar funerary rites of Sikhism. The site is renowned for being the cremation-grounds of two Sikh gurus, Guru Hargobind, Guru Har Rai, and the ashes of Guru Har Krishan were dispersed at this location.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kiratpur Sahib
topPlaces.description: Kiratpur, also known as Kiratpur Sahib, (lit. "city of glory") is a town, just 30 km from Rupnagar city in Rupnagar district of Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Dadhi
topPlaces.description: Dadhi is a small village in Rupnagar district, Punjab, India, near the towns of Rupnagar city, Kiratpur Sahib and Anandpur Sahib. The village is situated on the bank of the Sutlej River about 10 km (6.2 mi) south of Anandpur, 30 km (19 mi) north of Rupnagar and 90 km (56 mi) from Chandigarh on the Nangal-Rupnagar-Chandigarh road (National Highway 21).

topPlaces.name: Sarthali
topPlaces.description: Sarthali is a village in Rupnagar District in the Indian state of Punjab. It is under the Lok Sabha constituency of Anandpur Sahib, as well as under the Vidhan sabha constituency of Ropar.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Qila Anandgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Qila Anandgarh Sahib, alternatively spelt as Anandgarh Qila (meaning "fort of bliss"), is a gurdwara and qila fortress in the city of Anandpur Sahib, Rupnagar district, Punjab, India. Located near Virasat-e-Khalsa museum, it was one of the five forts of Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Guru of Sikhs.

topPlaces.name: Virasat-e-Khalsa
topPlaces.description: Virasat-e-Khalsa, also known as the Khalsa Heritage Complex, is a mega-museum complex of Sikhism, located in the town of Anandpur Sahib, and is close to the Takht Kesgarh Sahib, located in the state of Punjab, India. The museum celebrates 500 years of Sikh history and the 300th anniversary of the birth of Khalsa, based on the scriptures written by the tenth and last of Sikhism's gurus, Guru Gobind Singh.

topPlaces.name: Takht Kesgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Kesgarh Qila or Takht Kesgarh Sahib, alternatively spelt as Keshgarh Qila, is one of the five takhts of the Sikhs located in Anandpur Sahib in Rupnagar district of Punjab, India. It is located just 40 km from Rupnagar city, the district headquarters and 78 km from state capital Chandigarh.

topPlaces.name: Anandpur Sahib
topPlaces.description: Anandpur Sahib, also referred simply as Anandpur (lit. 'city of bliss'), is a city in Rupnagar district (Ropar), on the edge of Shivalik Hills, in the Indian state of Punjab. Located near the Sutlej River, the city is one of the most sacred religious places in Sikhism, being the place where the last two Sikh Gurus, Guru Tegh Bahadur and Guru Gobind Singh, lived.

topPlaces.name: Chak, Kapurthala
topPlaces.description: Chak is a village in Kapurthala district of Punjab State, India. It is located 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kapurthala, which is both district and sub-district headquarters of Chak.

items.note: Kiratpur, also known as Kiratpur Sahib, (lit. "city of glory") is a town, just 30 km from Rupnagar city in Rupnagar district of Punjab, India.

Dadhi is a small village in Rupnagar district, Punjab, India, near the towns of Rupnagar city, Kiratpur Sahib and Anandpur Sahib. The village is situated on the bank of the Sutlej River about 10 km (6.2 mi) south of Anandpur, 30 km (19 mi) north of Rupnagar and 90 km (56 mi) from Chandigarh on the Nangal-Rupnagar-Chandigarh road (National Highway 21).
Sarthali is a village in Rupnagar District in the Indian state of Punjab. It is under the Lok Sabha constituency of Anandpur Sahib, as well as under the Vidhan sabha constituency of Ropar.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Gurdwara Qila Anandgarh Sahib & around

items.note: Gurdwara Qila Anandgarh Sahib, alternatively spelt as Anandgarh Qila (meaning "fort of bliss"), is a gurdwara and qila fortress in the city of Anandpur Sahib, Rupnagar district, Punjab, India. Located near Virasat-e-Khalsa museum, it was one of the five forts of Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Guru of Sikhs.

Virasat-e-Khalsa, also known as the Khalsa Heritage Complex, is a mega-museum complex of Sikhism, located in the town of Anandpur Sahib, and is close to the Takht Kesgarh Sahib, located in the state of Punjab, India. The museum celebrates 500 years of Sikh history and the 300th anniversary of the birth of Khalsa, based on the scriptures written by the tenth and last of Sikhism's gurus, Guru Gobind Singh.

Kesgarh Qila or Takht Kesgarh Sahib, alternatively spelt as Keshgarh Qila, is one of the five takhts of the Sikhs located in Anandpur Sahib in Rupnagar district of Punjab, India. It is located just 40 km from Rupnagar city, the district headquarters and 78 km from state capital Chandigarh.

Anandpur Sahib, also referred simply as Anandpur (lit. 'city of bliss'), is a city in Rupnagar district (Ropar), on the edge of Shivalik Hills, in the Indian state of Punjab. Located near the Sutlej River, the city is one of the most sacred religious places in Sikhism, being the place where the last two Sikh Gurus, Guru Tegh Bahadur and Guru Gobind Singh, lived.
Chak is a village in Kapurthala district of Punjab State, India. It is located 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kapurthala, which is both district and sub-district headquarters of Chak.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Patalpuri Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kiratpur Sahib, Dadhi, Sarthali, Gurdwara Qila Anandgarh Sahib, Virasat-e-Khalsa.
